FluentGpu
Table of Contents

Namespace FluentGpu.Scene

Classes

ExtentTable

Per-item extent table for variable-height virtualization: a Fenwick tree / Binary Indexed Tree giving O(log n) OffsetOf(int) (prefix-sum → position a row) and IndexAt(float) (binary-lift → which item is at a scroll offset), with estimate-then-correct extents (an unmeasured item uses the estimate; on realize+measure SetExtent(int, float) corrects it, O(log n)). It persists across frames (only deltas update) and survives a fling because corrections are O(measured-rows). 100k items × 8B ≈ 800KB — within budget.

(virtualization.md §6.2 / layout.md §8.4 — the table MATH. It lives here in FluentGpu.Scene because both Layout and the Reconciler depend on Scene; double-precision prefix sums keep it exact at 100k×100px.)

FakeImageDecoder

Deterministic headless/offline decoder: completes on the NEXT Pump() (the +1-frame latency contract), reporting the requested target size and synthesizing a stable per-id BGRA pattern so the GPU upload + sample path is exercisable without real codecs or network. Proves the residency/state logic.

GridVirtualLayout

Uniform 2-D grid (album/artist card shelves) — virtualizes by ROW: realizes visible-rows × columns, recycles identically. Columns share the cross size; this is the virtualized UniformGridLayout.

GroupedListVirtualLayout

Grouped flat list with measured rows (E11-L0 grouping hook): the items are a FLATTENED projection — group headers occupy indices of their own (a header is just a measured item KIND, the app-requirements "Liked Songs" shape). HeaderIndices must be sorted ascending. Headers seed at HeaderEstimate, items at ItemEstimate; both correct to measured extents. StickyHeaderIndexAt(float) is the sticky-header support hook: the header that should pin for a given scroll offset (binary search, O(log groups)) — consumers (ItemsView / a custom list) translate that header's realized node by the pin delta at phase 7 (NodeFlags.StickyPinned). Vertical scroll. STATEFUL — create once and reuse (see MeasuredStackVirtualLayout).

HorizontalGridVirtualLayout

Uniform 2-D grid scrolling HORIZONTALLY (a shelf of fixed-width columns, Rows cells tall) — virtualizes by COLUMN: realizes visible-columns × rows. The horizontal variant of GridVirtualLayout; pair with VirtualListEl.Horizontal = true.

ImageCache

Portable image residency: source→handle dedup, a Pending/Ready/Failed state machine, liveness ref-counting (a pinned/on-screen image is never evicted — the single biggest real-world cache bug, per the research), and an LRU byte budget over decoded images. Decode happens off-thread behind IImageDecoder; Pump() applies completions (+1-frame latency). The GPU texture upload is a needs-pixels leaf concern; this owns the logic.

LinedFlowLayout

WinUI LinedFlowLayout — the ItemsView photo-wall: items flow left-to-right into uniform-height LINES, each item's width = its aspect ratio × LineHeight; a line wraps when the next item would overflow the cross size. Uniform line stride keeps windowing O(1); the per-item line table is (re)built lazily ONLY when (itemCount, crossSize) change — realize/arrange calls stay allocation-free per the seam contract. WinUI defaults: LineSpacing 0, MinItemSpacing 0 (LinedFlowLayout.h:25,27 s_defaultLineSpacing/s_defaultMinItemSpacing); LineHeight has no auto here (WinUI s_defaultLineHeight = NaN = auto-from-first-item; the engine takes it explicitly). Items wider than the cross size clamp to it (single-item line). STATEFUL — create once and reuse.

MeasuredStackVirtualLayout

The Fenwick variable-extent list behind the IMeasuredVirtualLayout seam (E11-L0): every row starts at Estimate, gets corrected to its measured extent on realize, and the layout engine re-pins the scroll anchor across corrections. Functionally the engine's legacy Layout = null variable path (kept for compatibility), but USER-REACHABLE: compose it, subclass the idea, or build your own measured layout on the same contract. STATEFUL (owns an ExtentTable) — create ONCE and reuse across renders (hoist in a UseMemo); the table self-rebuilds only on item-count change.

SceneStore

Struct-of-arrays retained RenderNode tree. One spine (gen + free-list) indexes all parallel columns.

SpanningGridVirtualLayout

Uniform-row grid with ITEM SPANNING (E11-L0): each item occupies SpanOf(i) columns (clamped 1..Columns) and items pack row-major — a span that doesn't fit the current row wraps to the next (the "hero as the first full-width row" shape: SpanOf(0) == Columns). Placement depends only on the item count (not cross size), so the table rebuilds only on count change; windowing binary-searches the monotonic row column. STATEFUL — create once and reuse.

StackVirtualLayout

Uniform 1-D stack (the WaveeMusic track-list shape) — O(1) windowing. horizontal scrolls X.

VirtualWindowing

Decides whether a scroll offset needs a virtual-window refresh, using the realized overscan as a guard band.

Structs

BrushAnim

An implicit brush transition (WinUI BrushTransition, 83ms): when a LOGICAL state flip re-renders a node with a different Fill/BorderColor/TextColor and the element opted in (BrushTransitionMs), the reconciler captures the previously-DISPLAYED color here and the recorder cross-fades from it to the new resolved color as T advances (linear-light, like the hover/press cross-fade). Sparse side-table — O(transitioning nodes), 0-alloc steady frames.

GridSpec

Grid layout spec for a grid container node (sparse side-table, O(grids)). The reconciler writes it from a GridEl; the layout engine resolves column tracks at the final width and auto-flows the cells row-major.

ImageBinding

What UseImage returns (media-pipeline.md §5): the stable handle + current load state, so a component can render a spinner / a broken-art fallback / read the failure kind, all without prop-drilling.

ImageHandle

A generational-free image handle into the ImageCache (0 = none).

InteractionAnim

Eased interaction progress for a node (sparse side-table, O(interacted nodes)). The InteractionAnimator eases HoverT/PressT toward their targets on pointer enter/leave/press; the recorder lerps Fill/Border with them for the WinUI ~83ms brush transition (instead of the instant flag switch).

InteractionInfo

Hit-test / input column.

LayoutInput

Layout-input column (flexbox: direction + gap + padding + margin + flex grow/shrink/basis + justify/align + min/max + explicit size + text style).

NodePaint

Paint column — one cache line of per-node visual state read by the record phase.

ScrollState

Scroll + virtualization state for a viewport node (marked NodeFlags.Scrollable). There are O(viewports) of these — not one per node — so the store keeps them in a sparse side-table keyed by node index, not a parallel column. Ownership (layout.md §6 / architecture-spec §5.5): Input owns Offset* (clamped to the published content); Layout publishes Content*/Viewport*; the virtualizer owns the Item* / realized-range / anchor fields. Scroll is layout-free: the -ScrollOffset translation is the ContentNode's LocalTransform, never a relayout.

TextEditState

Sparse text-edit state for an editor's TEXT node (side-table, O(editors)): caret geometry + caret-follow scroll + in-flight IME composition span + focus/blink flags. Written by the editing control (UI thread, edit/drag time) and the CaretBlinker phase-7 ticker; the recorder only READS it (plus the pooled decoration rects on SetTextEditRects(NodeHandle, ReadOnlySpan<RectF>, ReadOnlySpan<RectF>)) to emit selection highlight / selected-text recolor / IME underlines / the caret bar — retained scene state, never composed elements (0 alloc in phases 6–13).

TextMeasureCache

Per-text-node measure cache (layout.md §2.3): a pure-function cache of (text, style, availWidth) → size, so a scoped relayout skips re-shaping a text leaf whose inputs are unchanged. Self-invalidating — any input change makes the stored key not match. Helps the real DirectWrite shaping path; neutral for the headless fake font. Besides the size, the cache retains the face's DECORATION metrics from the same TextMetrics (top-down DIP, the line frame of Baseline — see FluentGpu.Text.TextMetrics): the recorder reads them at record time to place underline/strikethrough bars (NodePaint.TextDecorations) without re-touching the font seam. Filled by the layout engine's measure-miss path; 0 ⇒ the backend reported no face metrics (the recorder falls back to a size-derived approximation).

Interfaces

IImageDecoder

The decode seam: the portable cache asks a leaf to decode a source to a target size, off the UI thread. The Windows leaf is WIC→GPU (needs-pixels); the headless leaf is deterministic. Pump(ImageCompleteHandler, ImageReadyHandler) drains completions onto the UI thread (the +1-frame latency contract: a request is never ready the same frame), invoking onPixels with the bucket pixels then onComplete with the state transition.

IMeasuredVirtualLayout

The variable-extent (measured) virtualization seam — E11-L0. Folds the Fenwick estimate-then-correct path behind the SAME pluggable seam as the fixed-geometry layouts, so CUSTOM layouts can be variable/sliver-like: unmeasured items report their estimate; at arrange time the layout engine measures each realized row and feeds the real extent back through SetMeasured(int, float, float) (O(log n) correction), then re-pins the scroll anchor via IndexAt(float, float)/OffsetOf(int, float) so corrections above the viewport never jump the visible top (virtualization.md §6.2 — estimate-then-correct + anchoring are seam behavior now, not a special case). Built-ins: MeasuredStackVirtualLayout (the Fenwick variable list), GroupedListVirtualLayout (group headers = a measured item kind + sticky-header hook).

ISceneBackend

The reconciler's only window onto the retained tree — handle-in / handle-out, POD-only. (The slice implements this directly on SceneStore.)

IVirtualLayout

A pluggable virtualizing layout: PURE, ALLOCATION-FREE arithmetic mapping (itemCount, viewport, scrollOffset) to the realize window + per-item content-space rects. The engine calls it ONLY on realize/arrange frames (a steady in-window scroll is transform-only and never touches it), and the methods return structs / via out-params — so a custom layout costs zero per-frame managed allocation, honoring the engine's core contract. Implement this to make any virtualized layout (lists, card grids, staggered walls, …). Built-ins: StackVirtualLayout, GridVirtualLayout, HorizontalGridVirtualLayout, LinedFlowLayout, SpanningGridVirtualLayout. Variable-extent (measured) layouts implement the widened seam IMeasuredVirtualLayout — same contract plus estimate-then-correct feedback.

Enums

ImageFailureKind

Why an image decode ended (for diagnostics + app fallbacks). Transient kinds are retried by the decoder before a request is reported Failed; permanent kinds fail immediately.

ImagePriority

Decode urgency (media-pipeline.md §3). Workers drain higher priority first; under backpressure the lowest off-screen lane (Prefetch, then Overscan) is dropped — never Visible.

ImageState
VisualKind

Delegates

ImageCompleteHandler

Decode-completion callback: id, success, decoded dims, the failure kind (if any), and the fetch attempt count.

ImageReadyHandler

Forwards decoded PREMULTIPLIED BGRA8 pixels to the GPU backend. The span is valid only for the duration of the synchronous call (it is never stored — the backend copies it into its upload heap), so the cache need not own pixel memory: it flows decoder → cache.Pump → host sink → IGpuDevice.UploadImage in one stack.

ImageStatusHandler

Image lifecycle notification for app observability (e.g. a retry toast, a broken-art fallback, telemetry). Raised on the UI thread during Pump(). attempts is the fetch attempt count (≥1; >1 means transient retries happened).

FluentGpu — a near-zero-allocation, NativeAOT, GPU-rendered UI engine for .NET 10. Usage docs live here; architecture canon lives in design/ (SPEC-INDEX.md is the precedence authority).
